title.en: The Red Bean Porridge Grandma and the Tiger

summary: A cozy winter folktale where a pushy tiger demands red bean porridge, and Grandma’s tiny kitchen helpers earn spoonfuls by planning a clever, comic rescue.
sensitivityNotes: The tiger is boastful rather than dangerous.
Household helpers use slapstick tricks, not harm.
The ending centers sharing and repair.

text.enLittle: Snow tucked the village under a white blanket.
text.enStandard: Snow tucked the village under a white blanket. In a small kitchen, Grandma stirred red bean porridge until it bubbled like little drums.
text.koStandard: 눈이 마을을 하얀 이불처럼 덮었어요. 작은 부엌에서 할멈은 팥죽을 저었고, 팥죽은 작은 북처럼 보글보글 끓었지요.

pages.koreanText: 눈이 마을을 하얀 이불처럼 덮었어요. 작은 부엌에서 할멈은 팥죽을 저었고, 팥죽은 작은 북처럼 보글보글 끓었지요.
pages.englishText: Snow tucked the village under a white blanket. In a small kitchen, Grandma stirred red bean porridge until it bubbled like little drums.

text.enLittle: From outside came a rumble.
text.enStandard: From outside came a rumble. “Grandma! I smelled porridge, and I am very good at eating things that are not mine.”
text.koStandard: 밖에서 우르릉 소리가 들렸어요. “할멈아! 팥죽 냄새가 난다. 나는 남의 것도 아주 잘 먹는다!”

pages.koreanText: 밖에서 우르릉 소리가 들렸어요. “할멈아! 팥죽 냄새가 난다. 나는 남의 것도 아주 잘 먹는다!”

text.enLittle: Grandma held the ladle like a tiny moon.
text.enStandard: Grandma held the ladle like a tiny moon. “This porridge is for sharing,” she said, “not for snatching.”

text.enLittle: The tiger thumped the door.
text.enStandard: The tiger thumped the door. “Then I will come at moonrise!” The bowls rattled. Grandma’s spoon trembled once.
text.koStandard: 호랑이가 문을 쿵 쳤어요. “그럼 달 뜰 때 내가 들어가겠다!” 그릇들이 달그락거리고 할멈의 숟가락이 한 번 떨렸지요.

text.enLittle: A softshell turtle peeked from the water jar.

text.enLittle: Moonrise came.

text.koStandard: 그리고 무례한 손님이 다시 문을 쿵쿵 두드린다면, 할멈은 무슨 말을 해야 할지 알았어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.”

pages.koreanText: 그리고 무례한 손님이 다시 문을 쿵쿵 두드린다면, 할멈은 무슨 말을 해야 할지 알았어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.”
pages.englishText: And if a rude guest ever thumped at the door again, Grandma knew just what to say: “One warm spoonful, one clever plan.”
pages.narrationScript: 그리고 무례한 손님이 다시 문을 쿵쿵 두드린다면, 할멈은 무슨 말을 해야 할지 알았어요. “따뜻한 한 숟가락, 슬기로운 한 가지 꾀.”
pages.refrain: One warm spoonful, one clever plan!

vocabulary.definitionEn: The raised edge at the bottom of a doorway.
One scoop taken with a spoon.

storyBeat.pageTurnHook: The refrain closes the book. The ending invites a quiet reread and one last echo of the refrain.
storyBeat.readAloudCue: Read with cozy, brave, worried, proud, and comic; let teamwork feel warm and clever; keep pauses clear and musical. invite final repeat
storyBeat.childInteraction: Tap the door, then the spoon.

pages.imagePrompt: Cozy cottage door in snow, warm light inside, spoon and porridge pot glowing, confident ending Premium Korean watercolor, gouache, soft ink, hanji paper texture, child-safe ages 3-8, no text, no letters, no watermark.
pages.audioPrompt: English-first warm professional picture-book narration with confident coziness; Korean optional narration should sound natural, musical, and culturally grounded. Read-aloud cue: invite final repeat

layers.name: particle_glow
layers.motion: opacity pulse or slow particle drift
